pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 17 hardcoded value; verifier.py accepts this from metadata
setup_task.sh line 64; Web search confirms this is the address of Grand Sierra Resort and Casino in Reno, NV; Zillow and LoopNet list this address; verifier.py line 29 uses this as default
setup_task.sh line 65; Web search confirms Reno is a real city in Nevada; verifier.py line 30 uses this as default
setup_task.sh line 66; NV is the standard abbreviation for Nevada

setup_task.sh line 67; Web search confirms 89595 is a valid zip code for Reno, Nevada. Specifically, it is a specialized single-entity zip code assigned to the Grand Sierra Resort and Casino; verifier.py line 31 uses this as default

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic complaint case titled 'HazMat-Spill-2024-001' via the ArkCase API and instructs an agent to add a structured incident location to it. The specified incident location is: 2500 East Second Street, Reno, NV 89595 (the address of the Grand Sierra Resort and Casino). All address components (street, city, state, and zip code) are real, verified locations in Reno, Nevada. The case title is synthetic and does not correspond to any documented public incident. The data is programmatically generated by hardcoded values in the setup script and is not derived from an external dataset.
